title: Coq au vin
sources: Auguste Escoffier, Le Guide Culinaire (1903) — public domain — chicken braised in red wine recipes.
Earlier French regional sources (pre-1928) describing peasant origins in Burgundy and Auvergne; canonical version stabilised by Escoffier.
notes: The French anchor recipe. Bone-in chicken pieces marinated in red wine, browned, braised with lardons, button mushrooms, pearl onions. Mother-sauce-adjacent through the technique of beurre manié to finish. Burgundy origin, often Pinot Noir. Make a day ahead is better.
